title: Chart to track yoy sales and deals
excerpt:
Hi - in dashboards how would I go about building a chart that counted and summed all closed won deals by month from 2021 and also pulled in the same for 2022 so that I could see where we are tracking YOY? 
Thank you

posts.author: brianwbates (Brian Bates)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi - in dashboards how would I go about building a chart that counted and summed all closed won deals by month from 2021 and also pulled in the same for 2022 so that I could see where we are tracking YOY?

Thank you

posts.author: Cleopatra-upstream (Cleopatra Luneburg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @brianwbates

Welcome to the monday Community!

We have been partnered with monday.com since 2017.

We specialise in building custom workflows, integrations, automations and optimisation sessions to make monday.com platform even more powerful within your workspace.

Without seeing the types of columns or information in the columns you have on your boards.

The general way to display the information in questions would be through the settings of the widget in the dashboard to ensure it is picking up the information you need for closed won deals by month etc.

If you are unable to do this, you might need to restructure your columns/data in the board itself where the dashboard is grabbing the information.

Please book in a complimentary non obligation session here to discuss further.

We would love to hear more on what you are trying to achieve and help you achieve your goals on the platform.

Kind Regards,
Cleopatra Luneburg
upstream

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @brianwbates

This will largely depend on the way you’ve set up your boards.
Are you perhaps able to share with us a screenshot of the board you’re using to track your deals?

You can duplicate the board and replace any sensitive data with mock data, or you can blank out the sensitive data.

If you’re using a status column to track your deal status, along with a date column for the deal, you should be able to create a chart that compares these fairly easily.
For example:

image1258×1076 21.6 KB

This example would pull through each of the status labels and the number of items with that status label per year.
The settings would look something like this:

image760×784 19.6 KB
image762×726 23.4 KB

image758×538 20.5 KB

Could this be an option for you?

I hope this helps! Remember to mark as solved if this answers your question

Best,

Dani

posts.author: brianwbates (Brian Bates)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Dani,

Thank you for your reply. Here is what I have so far, which is comparing cumulative YOY when what I need is YOY by month.

Is there a way to design that in settings? Thanks
